content:
- All required gates passed: make docs-build, make check, make test, make test-long, and make lint.
- Marked the active pgtm config-backed operator-context task as done with passes=true and ran /bin/bash .ralph/task_switch.sh.
- Next is repo closeout only: stage all modified files including .ralph updates, commit with the required task-finished message summarizing the implementation and gate evidence, push, and stop.

- Repository closeout is complete.
- Committed the finished pgtm config-backed operator-context task as 6bf53dc with the required task-finished message including gate evidence and implementation summary.
- Post-commit docs publish succeeded and pushed docs repo commit ee557a8c; main repo push also succeeded.
- No AGENTS.md update was necessary; stopping now.

- Read `.ralph/current_tasks.md` and `.ralph/current_tasks_done.md` after loading prior progress.
- Confirmed there are no active failing bug tasks, then checked the candidate task files for hidden `<blocked_by>` tags and found none in the current active set.
- Selected `.ralph/tasks/story-ctl-operator-experience/03-task-add-cluster-wide-status-topology-and-table-output.md` as the highest-priority unblocked failing task because all remaining `medium` tasks outrank the `low` story, and this is the next unfinished task in the already-progressed operator-experience chain. Next step is to write that exact path into `.ralph/current_task.txt` and stop.

- Read the previous progress log and current task pointer.
- Active task is .ralph/tasks/story-ctl-operator-experience/03-task-add-cluster-wide-status-topology-and-table-output.md.
- Opening the task file now to determine whether this turn should plan, skeptically verify, or execute.

- Confirmed the active task is still pre-plan and must stop after writing a detailed plan plus TO BE VERIFIED.
- Read current CLI modules, API payloads, binary tests, docs, and HA multi-node harness to make the plan concrete.
- Key findings: status is single-node only, text output is fixed key=value, pgtm still requires explicit subcommand, and docs still tell operators to loop over nodes manually.

- Wrote a full execution plan into the active cluster-wide status task file and ended it with TO BE VERIFIED.
- The plan explicitly changes the CLI to default bare pgtm to status, replaces status output with a human table plus --json, adds --watch and -v, and calls out a new stable cluster discovery payload.
- Most important design choice recorded for the next engineer: cluster-wide status needs peer API discovery, so the plan adds node-advertised API targets to stable member data instead of relying on debug endpoints or manual loops.
- Stopping now at the required planning checkpoint without beginning implementation.

- Read previous progress log and confirmed the active task path from .ralph/current_task.txt.
- Active task is .ralph/tasks/story-ctl-operator-experience/03-task-add-cluster-wide-status-topology-and-table-output.md and prior turn already ended the plan with TO BE VERIFIED.
- Reading the task file now to perform the required skeptical plan review before any implementation.

- Loaded the task file and confirmed it ends in TO BE VERIFIED, so this turn must review and alter the plan before execution.
- Starting a skeptical codebase check against current CLI/API/docs/tests to validate the plan and force at least one concrete plan change.
- Looking specifically for whether a new /ha/cluster endpoint is actually the right move, or whether the existing stable surface can be extended more cleanly.

- Verified current code paths: CLI still only knows /ha/state and uses global --output {json,text}; there is no cluster payload yet.
- Skeptical review is converging on two concrete plan changes: prefer extending stable /ha/state instead of adding a brand-new /ha/cluster endpoint, and reuse existing pgtm.api_url as the advertised peer API target instead of inventing another config knob unless code forces it.
- Reading parser/docs references now to confirm those existing semantics are strong enough to support the plan change.

- Completed the required skeptical review of the task plan and changed it materially before execution.
- Concrete plan changes written into the task file: prefer extending stable /ha/state instead of adding a separate /ha/cluster endpoint, and reuse existing [pgtm].api_url as the node-advertised peer API target instead of creating a new config knob unless forced.
- Replaced TO BE VERIFIED with NOW EXECUTE and am moving directly into implementation without more broad exploration.

- Implemented the core architectural shift: stable /ha/state now carries cluster member discovery data, DCS member records publish api_url, and the CLI has a new status aggregation path plus bare-pgtm/default-status semantics.
- Replaced the old output surface with --json plus human table rendering, added watch plumbing, and started updating the status/switchover execution split.
- Most of the current time is in compiler fallout and fixture repairs from the new MemberRecord/HaStateResponse/CLI shapes; also had to cargo clean because the target dir hit a missing-object linker failure.

- Worker branch: docs-only pass for cluster-wide pgtm status.
- Read pgtm CLI reference, HTTP API reference, check-cluster-health, and perform-switchover docs; confirmed they still describe --output and single-node/manual-compare status behavior.
- Skipping k2-docs-loop because this is a targeted stale-doc correction across existing pages rather than a K2-led single-page drafting loop.

- Rewrote the main operator docs pages for the new cluster-wide pgtm status UX and patched the remaining stale examples that still used --output.
- Core doc changes now describe bare pgtm defaulting to status, human table output, --json, -v, --watch, queried-via semantics, and /ha/state member discovery data.
- Doing a final stale-reference sweep now before reporting back to the parent agent.

- Resumed the cluster-wide status task from the previous context window.
- Reloaded the Ralph progress log, confirmed the active task file, and checked git status to verify the pending code/docs/task-file edits are intact.
- Moving directly into final verification now: finish make test-long, then run make lint, then update task metadata and do repo closeout only if every gate passes.

- Started a fresh make test-long run to get a canonical full-suite result instead of assuming the interrupted docker-smoke phase had completed.
- The run reused warm artifacts quickly and is now inside the compose/docker smoke stages, which are the only remaining uncertain acceptance gates.
- Once test-long is green I will run make lint immediately and only then update the task file and close the repo out.

- make test-long finished cleanly on the canonical Makefile path.
- Confirmed ultra-long nextest, compose config validation, docker smoke single, and docker smoke cluster all passed in one run.
- Running make lint now; if it stays green I can mark the task as passing and do the required task-switch/commit/push closeout.

- make lint failed on docs policy rather than code: a how-to page used a ```json fenced block, which the repo disallows in that docs section.
- Patched docs/src/how-to/perform-switchover.md to keep the example but switch the fence to an allowed type.
- Rerunning make lint now; if that passes the remaining work is task metadata and repo closeout only.

- The first lint rerun got past docs and then failed on a single clippy warning in src/cli/status.rs: write! with a trailing newline in watch rendering.
- Patched that call to writeln! so the watch path stays identical but satisfies -D warnings.
- Rerunning make lint now; once this passes, all required gates should be green.

- make lint is now fully green, including docs-lint and all strict clippy restriction passes.
- Because I made a docs-policy fix and a small watch-renderer clippy fix after the earlier check/test success, I am rerunning make check and make test against the final tree before marking the task done.
- This keeps the closeout evidence aligned with the exact commit contents instead of relying on earlier pre-fix runs.

- Final gates are green on the ship candidate: make check, make test, make test-long, and make lint all passed.
- Updated the active task file to completed, checked every acceptance box, and set passes=true.
- Running the required Ralph closeout sequence now: task switch, stage all changes including .ralph, commit with gate evidence in the message, push, and stop.
